Merge #583

583: Port TurbulenceConvection to ClimaAtmos r=charleskawczynski a=charleskawczynski

There was a decision made in the recent EDMF meeting to port TurbulenceConvection into ClimaAtmos in order to smooth development of several pieces that need to be updated together:

 - Using consistent energy between grid mean and updraft variables
 - Using a fully compressible model
 - Using an implicit solver

These pieces cannot be (easily) done in TC alone because TC has a built-in anelastic assumption, which is incompatible with the energy formulation.

If what I've wrote is somehow inaccurate, please chime in, `@yairchn,` `@tapios.`

Once we merge this, we can add a note/warning in the TC readme indicating that development has been moved to this repo.

Co-authored-by: Charles Kawczynski <kawczynski.charles@gmail.com>

Remove Distributions

Distributions is a heavy package, but all we need is the inverse error
function. Wikipedia teaches us that there are relatively simple
approximations for that:

https://www.academia.edu/9730974/A_handy_approximation_for_the_error_function_and_its_inverse

Fix compat and add downgrade action

The compats in ClimaAtmos were largely wrong. In this commit, I fix them. I also
add a new action that checks that our tests pass with the oldest possible
combination of packages that is advertised to be compatible with ClimaAtmos.

Since we required ClimaComms 0.6, I also removed all the checks on that version
for the import_required_backends

Refactor of Atmos unit tests, Part I (#4221)

* Test re-org and added utilities.jl tests

* More test refactors/additions

* More test refactors/additions

* Formatting

* Fix GPU compatibility and advection function call bug

Bug fixes:
- Fix vertical_transport → vertical_advection in EDMFX 2M microphysics
  The code was calling vertical_transport() with 3 args but all defined
  methods require 5 args (ᶜρ, ᶠu³, ᶜχ, dt, upwinding). Changed to
  vertical_advection() which takes 3 args for advective-form.
  This fixes MethodError in prognostic_edmfx_rico_column_2M and similar.

- Make thermo_vars GPU-compatible via multiple dispatch
  Converted single function with runtime 'isa' type checks to three
  dispatched methods for DryModel, EquilMoistModel, NonEquilMoistModel.
  Runtime dispatch causes jl_f_getfield calls which are GPU-incompatible.
  This fixes InvalidIRError in les_isdac_box and similar GPU tests.

Also restores test/implicit/debugging_tools.jl from main.

* Test cleanup: remove redundant tests and improve field operations

- Remove redundant damping threshold tests (already covered by ≈ tests)
- Replace magic numbers with computed expressions for clarity
- Use ClimaCore field broadcasting instead of fill!(parent(...))
- Add space-filling curve to topology constructor in test_helpers.jl
- Simplify assertions to test exact expected values

* Add MEMORY.md to .gitignore

* Guard against negative pressure in Jacobian evaluation

During Newton iterations with implicit microphysics + lognormal SGS,
the solver can temporarily produce states with negative pressure.
This caused DomainError in log(p) inside exner_given_pressure, called
from theta_v during update_jacobian!.

Fix: clamp ᶜp to eps(FT) in the Jacobian computation. The Jacobian
is an approximation (ManualSparseJacobian) and should never crash from
DomainError at unphysical intermediate Newton states. A finite Jacobian
at a bad state allows Newton to take a corrective step back toward
physical space.

Verified: DYCOMS RF02 with lognormal SGS + implicit microphysics now
completes the full 5-hour simulation without crash.

SGS inversion reconstruction: make EDMF (PROPHET!) vertically scale-aware (and stabilize clouds) (#4647)

* Reconstruct subgrid inversion structure in the EDMFX turbulence closure

Make the EDMFX (PROPHET) boundary-layer representation less sensitive to
vertical resolution by reconstructing the subgrid structure of a capping
inversion, instead of letting a centered, arithmetically-interpolated eddy
diffusivity erode it.

Stability side:
- Interpolate center-based eddy diffusivities to faces with a harmonic mean
  (`1/interp(1/max(K, ϵ))`) at the cell-center-diffusivity sites (the
  boundary-layer VerticalDiffusion/DecayWithHeightDiffusion path and EDMFX
  updraft-internal diffusion), so the flux collapses at a face separating a
  turbulent layer from quiescent stratified air instead of taking ~K/2 there.
- Use a stability-biased buoyancy gradient (the more stable of the two
  one-sided gradients, `N²_stab = max(N²₋, N²₊)`) for the mixing length and
  turbulent Prandtl number, so a resolved inversion suppresses mixing from
  both sides.
- Make the stability closure interface-aware: an effective
  `N²_eff = N² + [(Δb)₊]²/(c_b κ_iso)` senses unresolved buoyancy jumps
  across a face, and a gated interfacial entrainment diffusivity
  `K_e = γ w_e Δz` (with `w_e = A √κ / max(Ri_b, 1)`) restores the physical,
  finite entrainment across the reconstructed inversion. `A` is the new
  calibratable parameter `EDMF_interface_entr_efficiency`.

Numerics and structure:
- Evaluate the stability closure and eddy diffusivities natively at cell
  faces (`ᶠK_h`, `ᶠK_u`, `ᶠK_entr`, exact two-point differences), shared by
  the grid-mean and updraft-internal diffusion (tendencies and Jacobian);
  cache the master mixing length (`ᶜl_mix`); and factor the
  buoyancy-gradient thermodynamics into cloud-fraction-independent
  coefficients reused by the centered, one-sided, and face-native stencils.
  The TKE budget uses the same face diffusivities and face buoyancy gradient
  as the fluxes.
- Cap the mixing length by the resolvability filter scale
  `l ≤ Δ_f = max(Δx_h, Δz)` (horizontal node scale; `Inf` for single
  columns) instead of the vertical-only `l ≤ Δz`: an eddy is subgrid unless
  resolvable in every direction, so the cap is set by the coarsest grid
  direction — inert for single columns and GCM resolutions, binding at
  `Δx_h` in the gray zone, and Deardorff `l ≤ Δ` in the isotropic LES limit.
- Use the EDMFX mixing length for the diagnostic-covariance and ML-cloud
  closures under EDOnlyEDMFX (previously fell back to Smagorinsky–Lilly).
- Report the effective coefficients in the `edt`/`evu` diagnostics (the
  face-native values applied to the grid-mean fluxes, including the
  interfacial entrainment contribution, interpolated to centers) instead of
  recomputing a center-based diffusivity, and output the interfacial
  entrainment diffusivity as the new default EDMFX diagnostic `kentr`.
- Read `EDMF_interface_entr_efficiency` directly from ClimaParams v1.1.2.

Jacobian:
- Weight the interfacial entrainment on the sedimenting-tracer diffusion
  diagonal to `ρ(α K_h + K_e)`, matching the tendency's effective
  diffusivity (K_e crosses the interface at the same velocity for every
  scalar), so the block is not zeroed at inversions when α = 0.
- Allocate the density-weighted 0M microphysics sources
  (`ᶜρ_dq_tot_dt`/`ᶜρ_de_tot_dt`) in implicit_precomputed_quantities so the
  autodiff Jacobians get Dual-typed copies (the implicit refresh rewrites
  them from the Newton iterate); fixes a pre-existing AutoDenseJacobian
  crash on EquilibriumMicrophysics0M configurations.

Validity domain (documented in `set_face_diffusivities!`): the closure
targets strong, mixed-layer-capping inversions, where cloud cover and
inversion height converge under vertical refinement (DYCOMS, BOMEX); at
weak, moisture-dominated trade inversions on coarse grids the equilibrium
cloud cover remains resolution-dependent for any `A`, so `A` should be
calibrated against equilibrium (≳ 24 h) targets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add unit tests for the pointwise eddy-diffusion closures

Lock in the analytic structure of the new pointwise closures across
Float32/Float64: `blended_N²` and `buoyancy_gradient_coefficients`
(cf-linearity, dry limit, equivalence with the `buoyancy_gradients`
reference path, saturated-coefficient signs); `interface_effective_N²`
(exact jump term, resolved-limit smallness, inertness for unstable/neutral
gradients, monotonicity in Δz); `interface_entrainment_diffusivity`
(cubic `K_e ∝ Δz³` decay, Δz-independent sheet flux `K_e/Δz → w_e`,
`w_e ≤ A√κ` bound, inert cases); and the resolvability filter scale
(`horizontal_filter_scale` space dispatch, `resolvability_filter_scale`
pointwise and as a field, and the `max(Δx_h, Δz)` cap binding in the
mixing-length closure).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add NEWS entry and bump the reproducibility counter for #4647

Document the EDMFX inversion-reconstruction change (its bullets, the
`EDMF_interface_entr_efficiency` default of 0.4, and the closure's
validity domain) and bump the reproducibility reference counter to 381.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Optimize GPU stencils and close AD/Float32 physical consistency in inversion-recon

Performance and robustness pass over the inversion-reconstruction closure
(no change to the headline SCM results; ~16% faster per step):

- Cache the master mixing length once. When the configuration uses SGS
  (co)variances, ᶜl_mix is materialized inside the covariance/cloud-fraction
  iteration; otherwise the explicit stage writes it. The shared
  `uses_covariances(atmos)` predicate is the single source of truth that
  keeps the two paths from disagreeing, and `materialized_mixing_length!`
  is the single place that decides where ᶜl_mix lives — avoiding 2–4
  redundant evaluations of the parameter-heavy `mixing_length_lopez_gomez_2020`
  broadcast per step and keeping GPU kernel parameters under the size limit.
- Compute the centered vertical gradients of (θ_li, q_tot) in
  `set_buoyancy_gradient_inputs!`, reusing the once-materialized θ_li instead
  of re-evaluating the pow-heavy Exner function for every centered stencil.
- Branchless `ifelse` in the interfacial-entrainment gate and the
  energy-containing eddy scale (avoids GPU warp divergence).
- Let `ᶜmixing_length` fall back to zero TKE where prognostic TKE is absent,
  and access ρtke / ρtke_flux only inside the prognostic-TKE branch of
  `edmfx_sgs_diffusive_flux_tendency!`, so EDMF configurations without
  prognostic TKE no longer error.

Numerical refinements ("close AD/Float32 consistency"):

- Interfacial-entrainment gate γ = jt/(N²+jt) computed exactly wherever
  active (jt > 0 ⟹ N²_face > 0 ⟹ the denominator is positive, so no ε/floatmin
  guard is needed), removing the earlier ε-guard underestimate for tiny jt.
- Bound the energy-containing eddy scale ℓ_e by the resolvability filter
  scale l_grid where the grid imposes a finite one (inert for single
  columns, where l_grid = Inf; binds only in the gray zone / LES),
  consistent with the master mixing-length cap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review feedback and clarify buoyancy-gradient naming

Review-round cleanups on the inversion-reconstruction closure:

- Rename center buoyancy fields to mirror their face-native counterparts:
  ᶜlinear_buoygrad -> ᶜbuoygrad (same N² as ᶠbuoygrad), and
  ᶜbuoygrad_stab -> ᶜN²_eff (same effective stability as ᶠN²_eff, and
  matching the ᶜN²_eff argument name in the closure functions).
- Give ᶠK_entr a definite value on every explicit update: zero it in the
  else-branch of set_face_diffusivities! when the interface entrainment
  closure is off, so the face K fields (ᶠbuoygrad/ᶠK_h/ᶠK_u/ᶠK_entr) can be
  allocated with similar instead of zeros.
- Allocate the face K fields and ᶜl_mix only for AbstractEDMF; destructure
  ᶠK_h/ᶠK_u/ᶠK_entr inside the AbstractEDMF branches of the diffusion
  Jacobian (other closures use the center ᶜK_h/ᶜK_u).
- Simplify materialized_mixing_length! to two branches and drop the
  always-true has_field(ρtke) guards; revert the ᶜmixing_length tke
  fallback to plain specific (AbstractEDMF always carries ρtke).
- Combine the two edmfx_tke_tendency! methods into one on
  Union{EDOnlyEDMFX, PrognosticEDMFX} with the use_prognostic_tke guard.
- compute_lmix returns the cached ᶜl_mix for EDMF instead of recomputing.
- Remove kentr from the default diagnostics (still registered on request);
  give bgrad the long_name "Vertical Moist Buoyancy Gradient".
- Bump ClimaParams compat to 1.1.2 and drop a stale comment.
